content: We can reframe Self-Refine’s single-perspective iterative improvement framework as a blank structure that can be populated with the multiple inconsistent solving perspectives from LLM self-reflection mechanisms to create a hybrid self-improvement system that retains Self-Refine’s core advantage of no extra training or supervised data while addressing its limitation of blind spots from single-perspective feedback. This hybrid system would let an LLM first generate an initial output, then pull conflicting critical perspectives from its own alternative reasoning paths to generate multi-dimensional self-feedback in each iteration, rather than relying on the same single perspective to critique its own work.
